evals.prompt: Design a new module for handling provider notifications — sending emails and in-app alerts when a credential is about to expire. This would be owned by the same team that owns credentialing. We're on a modular monolith in Azure.
evals.expected_output: Architecture proposal with: problem statement, recommendation (stays in monolith, not a service), feature folder structure, Azure Functions for background processing, Azure Storage Queue for decoupling notification dispatch, Conway's law check affirming single-team ownership, deployment independence section. No repository pattern. No third-party NuGet unless justified.

evals.prompt: Review this architecture: we're building a new Blazor WASM feature for managing user roles. The engineer wants to create a new class library called Verifiable.Portal.RoleManagement.Services to house the service logic separately from the UI project.
evals.expected_output: Assessment that pushes back on the separate class library — it should be a feature folder inside Verifiable.Portal.Ui (Features/RoleManagement/Services/). Concerns section calling out unnecessary project proliferation. Recommends feature folder + namespace instead. Mentions the only valid exception (distributable package).

evals.prompt: I need to add a mapping library to our .NET project for transforming domain models to API response models. The team is used to AutoMapper. Should I use it?
evals.expected_output: Direct recommendation to NOT use AutoMapper — write the mapping methods directly (Microsoft-first / write-it-yourself principle). Calls out that AutoMapper is a third-party package without significant feature advantage over manual mapping. May offer a Microsoft alternative (e.g. manual extension methods). Notes license check would be required if a package were used.
